stem: Find the curved surface area of a cylinder with radius $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $10 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The curved surface area of a cylinder is $A = 2\pi r h$.

stem: Find the curved surface area of a cylinder with radius $5 \text{ cm}$ and height $8 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The curved surface area is $A = 2\pi r h$. Substitute $r = 5$ and $h = 8$.

stem: Find the curved surface area of a cylinder with radius $2 \text{ cm}$ and height $7 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
Find the curved surface area of a cylinder with radius $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $6 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The curved surface area of a cylinder is $A = 2\pi r h$. Multiply out and use $\pi \approx 3.14159$.

stem: Find the total surface area of a closed cylinder with radius $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $9 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: A closed cylinder has two circular ends and a curved surface: $TSA = 2\pi r h + 2\pi r^2$.

stem: Find the total surface area of a closed cylinder with radius $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $6 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: Use $TSA = 2\pi r h + 2\pi r^2$. The two circular ends each have area $\pi r^2$.

stem: Find the total surface area of a closed cylinder with radius $7 \text{ cm}$ and height $12 \text{ cm}$. Give your answer in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: Add the curved surface area and the two circular ends: $TSA = 2\pi r h + 2\pi r^2$.

stem: A closed cylindrical drum has a diameter of $10 \text{ cm}$ and a height of $15 \text{ cm}$. Find its total surface area in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The diameter is $10 \text{ cm}$, so the radius is $5 \text{ cm}$. Then use $TSA = 2\pi r h + 2\pi r^2$.

stem: A cylindrical storage tin has no lid. The radius is $5 \text{ cm}$ and the height is $12 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area of material needed to make the tin in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: An open-top cylinder has a curved surface and one circular base only: $SA = 2\pi r h + \pi r^2$.

stem: A label is to be wrapped tightly around the outside of a cylindrical can. The can has a diameter of $8 \text{ cm}$ and a height of $14 \text{ cm}$. Find the area of the label in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The label covers only the curved surface. Use $A = 2\pi r h$ with $r = 4$ (half the diameter).

stem: A length of metal pipe is open at both ends. It has a radius of $3 \text{ cm}$ and a length of $20 \text{ cm}$. Find the area of metal used to make the pipe in $\text{cm}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: A pipe open at both ends has no circular ends — only the curved surface: $A = 2\pi r h$.

stem: A concrete pillar is a solid cylinder with a diameter of $1.2 \text{ m}$ and a height of $3 \text{ m}$. Calculate the total surface area of the pillar (including both circular ends) in $\text{m}^2$ rounded to 2 decimal places.
hint: The radius is half the diameter: $r = 0.6 \text{ m}$. Use $TSA = 2\pi r h + 2\pi r^2$.
